---
title: "homework 10"
format: html
---
```{python}
### Homework 10 ### 
# Python Data Structures #
# Sofia Puccio 03/25/26 # 

# List Operations 

# First we need to create a mixed-type list 
import numpy as np

my_list = [42, "hello", True, [1, 2, 3, 4], 3.14]

# 1) Pull out the third element (index 2)
third_element = my_list[2]

# 2) Extract the interior list and get its 3rd element
inner_list = my_list[3]
listWithinList = inner_list[2]

# 3) Use list methods

# .append() adds an element to the end of the list
my_list.append("new item")

# .pop() removes and returns the last element (or a specified index)
removed_item = my_list.pop()

# -----------------------------
# 2) DICTIONARY OPERATIONS
# -----------------------------

# Create a dictionary
my_dict = {
    "name": "Sofia",
    "age": 21,
    "major": "Biology",
    "gpa": 3.8,
    "graduated": False
}

# 1) Extract keys
keys = my_dict.keys()

# 2) Extract values
values = my_dict.values()

# 3) Use dictionary methods

# .get() retrieves the value for a key without causing an error if it doesn't exist
name_value = my_dict.get("name")

# .pop() removes a key-value pair and returns its value
removed_value = my_dict.pop("graduated")

# 4) Add a new element
my_dict["minor"] = "Chemistry"

# 5) Remove a different element
del my_dict["gpa"]


# 3) NUMPY OPERATIONS

import numpy as np

# Create array of length 100 with values between 0 and 1
arr1 = np.random.rand(100)

# 1) Extract last 10 elements using negative indexing
last_10 = arr1[-10:]

# 2) Convert floats to strings, then back to floats
arr_str = arr1.astype(str)
arr_float = arr_str.astype(float)

# 3) Reshape into 10x10 array
arr2D = arr1.reshape(10, 10)

# 4) Create binary array with p(1)=0.2 and p(0)=0.8
arr_bin = np.random.choice([0, 1], size=(10, 10), p=[0.8, 0.2])

# 5) Stack into 3D array (10x10x2)
arr_3D = np.stack((arr2D, arr_bin), axis=2)

```
